_global.text:
## Summary
Build ToolShare as a greenfield full-stack TypeScript app: a React + Vite SPA (React Router) served by an Express + Prisma (PostgreSQL) API with JWT auth, covering roles, catalog, loans, reservations, and an overdue dashboard, packaged in one Docker image.

## Scope
Greenfield — repo contains only `README.md` and a Colossus deploy workflow. All application files are new.

### Files to create
**Root**
- `package.json` — npm workspaces (`client`, `server`), root scripts (`build`, `dev`, `seed`).
- `Dockerfile` — multi-stage: build client (`vite build` → `client/dist`), build server (tsc), copy `client/dist` into server image; server serves API + static SPA. Runs `prisma migrate deploy` + seed on start.
- `.dockerignore`, `.env.example` (`DATABASE_URL`, `JWT_SECRET`, `PORT`), `docker-compose.yml` (Postgres for local).

**Server (`server/`)**
- `package.json`, `tsconfig.json` — Express, Prisma, `@prisma/client`, `bcryptjs`, `jsonwebtoken`, `zod`.
- `prisma/schema.prisma` — models: `User(id, email unique, passwordHash, role enum ADMIN|USER, createdAt)`, `Tool(id, name, category enum Power|Hand|Garden|Measurement, condition enum New|Good|Fair|Worn, createdAt)`, `Loan(id, toolId, borrowerName, borrowerUserId?, dueDate, returnedAt?, createdAt)`, `Reservation(id, toolId, userId, status enum ACTIVE|FULFILLED|CANCELLED, createdAt)`.
- `prisma/seed.ts` — creates ADMIN + USER demo accounts (bcrypt-hashed), sample tools; prints one `SEED_CREDS_JSON {"admin":{"email":...,"password":...},"user":{...}}` line to stdout.
- `src/index.ts` — Express bootstrap, static SPA serving with catch-all to `index.html`.
- `src/prisma.ts` — Prisma client singleton.
- `src/auth/jwt.ts`, `src/auth/middleware.ts` — `requireAuth`, `requireAdmin` guards; JWT sign/verify.
- `src/routes/auth.ts` — `POST /api/auth/signup` (first user → ADMIN, rest → USER), `POST /api/auth/login`, `GET /api/auth/me`, `POST /api/auth/logout`.
- `src/routes/tools.ts` — `GET /api/tools` (search `q`, filter `category`, `status`; returns derived availability), `POST/PATCH/DELETE` (admin).
- `src/routes/loans.ts` — `GET /api/loans` (filter `status=active|overdue|returned`), `POST` (admin, create loan; marks tool on-loan), `POST /api/loans/:id/return` (admin).
- `src/routes/reservations.ts` — `GET /api/reservations`, `POST` (USER reserves on-loan tool).
- `src/routes/dashboard.ts` — `GET /api/dashboard/overdue` (tool, borrower, dueDate, daysOverdue).
- `src/routes/health.ts` — `GET /api/health`, `GET /api/health/deep` (DB ping).
- `src/lib/availability.ts` — derive `available|on_loan|reserved` from open loan + active reservation; compute `daysOverdue`.

**Client (`client/`)**
- `package.json`, `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig.json`, `index.html` — React 18 + TS + React Router 6; dev proxy `/api` → server.
- `src/main.tsx`, `src/App.tsx` — router with `data.flow` nodes per route.
- `src/api/client.ts` — fetch wrapper with JWT bearer from localStorage.
- `src/auth/AuthContext.tsx`, `src/auth/RequireAuth.tsx`, `src/auth/RequireAdmin.tsx` — guards.
- `src/components/Shell.tsx` — app shell header displaying "ToolShare" on every page.
- Pages: `LoginPage.tsx`, `SignupPage.tsx`, `DashboardPage.tsx` (Overdue section), `ToolsPage.tsx` (search/filter via query params, `?modal=new-tool|edit-tool&id=`, `?panel=<id>` detail), `LoansPage.tsx` (`?modal=new-loan`), `ReservationsPage.tsx`.

### Dependencies
- Server: `express`, `@prisma/client`, `prisma`, `bcryptjs`, `jsonwebtoken`, `zod`, `cors`, `typescript`, `tsx`.
- Client: `react`, `react-dom`, `react-router-dom`, `vite`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `typescript`.
- Infra: PostgreSQL (via `DATABASE_URL`).

### Integrations
- None (no third-party APIs or external services).

### Assumptions
- Stack chosen (no config present): React/Vite SPA + Express/Prisma/Postgres, single Docker image; JWT auth (React-family → JWT per baseline).
- Auth model = `full_auth`: signup/login/logout + guards; first signup becomes ADMIN; seed also creates demo ADMIN+USER. Public routes: `/login`, `/signup`, `/api/health`, `/api/health/deep`.
- Tool availability and `daysOverdue` are **derived** at query time, not stored, to avoid stale flags.
- Reservations are FIFO by `createdAt`; the earliest ACTIVE reservation is "next in line" and shown on the tool.
- Borrower on a loan is a free-text name (spec says "borrower"), optionally linked to a user id.

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Scaffold workspaces & tooling
**Files:** root `package.json`, `server/`, `client/` configs, `.env.example`.
**Change:** Set up npm workspaces, TypeScript configs, Vite dev proxy, root `build`/`dev`/`seed` scripts.

### Step 2: Prisma schema & migration
**File:** `server/prisma/schema.prisma`
**Change:** Define User/Tool/Loan/Reservation models + enums; generate initial migration.

### Step 3: Auth
**Files:** `server/src/auth/*`, `server/src/routes/auth.ts`
**Change:** bcrypt signup/login, JWT issue/verify, `requireAuth`/`requireAdmin`, `/me`. First user → ADMIN.

### Step 4: Availability helper
**File:** `server/src/lib/availability.ts`
**Change:** Compute status (available/on_loan/reserved) and daysOverdue from loans/reservations.

### Step 5: Tools API
**File:** `server/src/routes/tools.ts`
**Change:** Admin CRUD; list with `q`/`category`/`status` filters returning derived availability.

### Step 6: Loans API
**File:** `server/src/routes/loans.ts`
**Change:** Admin create loan (records borrower + dueDate, tool→on-loan), return endpoint (sets returnedAt, tool→available), list with status filter (active/overdue/returned).

### Step 7: Reservations API
**File:** `server/src/routes/reservations.ts`
**Change:** USER reserves an on-loan tool → ACTIVE reservation; list endpoint. On loan return, surface earliest ACTIVE reservation as next-in-line.

### Step 8: Dashboard + health
**Files:** `server/src/routes/dashboard.ts`, `server/src/routes/health.ts`
**Change:** Overdue list (tool, borrower, dueDate, daysOverdue); health/deep endpoints.

### Step 9: Seed
**File:** `server/prisma/seed.ts`
**Change:** Create demo ADMIN + USER + sample tools; print `SEED_CREDS_JSON` line to stdout. Idempotent (upsert by email).

### Step 10: Client shell, auth, routing
**Files:** `client/src/App.tsx`, `Shell.tsx`, `auth/*`
**Change:** Router with guards; shell header renders "ToolShare" on every page; login redirects to `/dashboard`.

### Step 11: Client pages
**Files:** pages listed above.
**Change:** Login/Signup; Dashboard "Overdue" section; Tools catalog with search/filter query params + admin add/edit modals + detail panel; Loans with new-loan modal + return action; Reservations list + reserve action. All navigable states URL-addressable.

### Step 12: Dockerfile & deploy wiring
**File:** `Dockerfile`
**Change:** Multi-stage build; server serves SPA + API; startup runs `prisma migrate deploy` then seed.

## Testing Strategy
- Auth: signup first user → ADMIN, login returns JWT, guarded routes 401 without token, admin-only routes 403 for USER.
- Catalog: admin creates "Cordless Drill" (Power/Good) → appears available; USER list shows name/category/condition/availability; search & filters narrow results.
- Loans: create loan → tool shows on-loan, appears in loans list with borrower + due date; return → loan closed, tool available.
- Reservations: USER reserves on-loan tool → appears in reservations list; earliest ACTIVE is next-in-line on return.
- Dashboard: past-due unreturned loan appears under "Overdue" with tool, borrower, due date, days overdue.
- Seed prints a parseable `SEED_CREDS_JSON` line; both demo accounts log in.
- Health/deep endpoints return 200; deep pings DB.
- Deep-link every route/query-param state directly (e.g. `/tools?category=Power&status=on_loan`, `/tools?modal=new-tool`).

## Risks
- Availability/overdue derivation must be consistent across tools, loans, and dashboard endpoints — centralize in the availability helper to avoid divergence.
- Concurrent reservations on the same tool — enforce single ACTIVE reservation per user per tool and FIFO ordering.
- Docker startup ordering: ensure `migrate deploy` + seed complete before the server accepts traffic; seed must be idempotent to survive restarts.

requirements.text: rename the Overdue dashboard heading to Overdue Watchlist
